articles.title: Stocks Steady at Highs as Traders Await Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: US stocks held steady at all-time highs as traders waited to see whether Washington and Iran could finally get a peace deal across the line and restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Deutsche Bank Hikes Chairman Pay Despite Investor Critique
articles.summary: Deutsche Bank AG’s shareholders approved a plan to give a big pay raise to Supervisory Board Chairman Alexander Wynaendts after criticism at the lender’s annual general meeting.

articles.title: French and Spanish Inflation Jump, Boosting Case for ECB Hike
articles.summary: Inflation in France and Spain accelerated to the fastest level since 2024, backing the case for the European Central Bank to raise interest rates.

articles.title: Euro-Zone Consumer Price Expectations May Rise More, ECB Warns
articles.summary: The Iran war may push medium-term inflation expectations among euro-area consumers higher, according to a European Central Bank blog post that appears to underpin the case for hiking interest rates.

articles.title: India Cuts Monsoon Outlook to 90% of Normal in Risk to Crops
articles.summary: India lowered this year’s monsoon forecast, a blow to farmers who are already facing higher input costs due to the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Oil Set for 19% Collapse in May as Traders Bet On Truce Renewal
articles.summary: Oil fell as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days, with Brent set for the biggest monthly drop since 2020 on optimism that flows through the Strait of Hormuz may resume.
